{{Chess Opening Theory/Position|= |Smith-Morra Gambit Accepted| |rd|nd|bd|qd|kd|bd|nd|rd|= |pd|pd| |pd|pd|pd|pd|pd|= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | | | | | |= | | | | |pl| | | |= | | |pd| | | | | |= |pl|pl| | | |pl|pl|pl|= |rl|nl|bl|ql|kl|bl|nl|rl|= || }} =Smith-Morra Gambit= Black accepted the gambit by taking the pawn on c3. Offering another pawn, by playing anything other than [[/4. Nxc3|4. Nxc3]], will be too much and black will get an advantage. Taking back with [[/4. bxc3|4. bxc3]] is inferior, since it blocks the natural developing square of the knight on b1. ==Theory table== {{Chess Opening Theory/Table}}. :'''1. e4 c5 2. d4 cxd4 3. c3 dxc3'''
